source.title: Interoception, homeostatic emotions and sympathovagal balance.
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

confidence_rationale: Authoritative review by Craig (the dominant figure on insular interoception) framing homeostatic emotions (hunger, thirst, pain, dyspnoea, fatigue) as primary motivational forces routed through posterior-to-anterior insula re-representation, with sympathovagal balance providing a unifying common-currency. The review's relevance to V3 is at the meta-architectural level: it argues the brain has an integrated homeostatic emotion system whose substrate is the insular cortex and whose outputs drive behaviour. The 'common currency' framing maps onto SD-032a's arbitration job. Confidence moderate-high because the source is authoritative but the translation to V3 is at the level of organising principle rather than specific circuit.
failure_signatures: Hunger, pain, dyspnoea integrated into a common interoceptive substrate -- supports V3 z_harm as a multi-source integration channel rather than per-modality silos.
Sympathovagal balance as common-currency -- there exists a low-dimensional axis on which all homeostatic emotions can be compared for arbitration.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Homeostatic emotions (hunger, thirst, pain, dyspnoea, temperature, fatigue) constitute a unified class of primary motivational signals, routed through posterior-to-anterior insular re-representation, with sympathovagal balance providing a common autonomic substrate. Each emotion has dedicated lamina-I afferent routing but is integrated into a common motivational currency.
mapping.ree_translation: For V3, this supports two specific design choices: (1) z_harm and drive_level should be modeled as members of a common homeostatic-emotion class with shared upstream substrate (posterior insula analog) rather than fully orthogonal channels; (2) SD-032a SalienceCoordinator's arbitration should operate on a sympathovagal-like common currency that allows comparison across drives even when their dedicated channels are distinct. This argues against the current V3 architecture's full orthogonality of z_harm and drive_level streams.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The common-currency framing is a theoretical organising principle; the specific neural implementation of cross-drive arbitration is not fully specified by Craig. The Strigo/Craig framework prioritises insular hierarchy as the organising substrate, which V3 does not explicitly model.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review on interoception and homeostatic emotion, building on Craig's prior work on lamina-I afferent organisation and insular re-representation.

confidence_components.notes: Authoritative review by Craig. Mapping fidelity moderate -- the framework is more architectural-organising-principle than specific circuit prediction.
